solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int tribonacci(int n) {
        if (n == 0) return 0;
        if (n == 1 || n == 2) return 1;

        int a = 0, b = 1, c = 1;
        for (int i = 3; i <= n; ++i) {
            int next = a + b + c;
            a = b;
            b = c;
            c = next;
        }
        return c;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses an iterative approach with constant space to compute the Tribonacci sequence. It avoids recursion and memoization, which reduces overhead and ensures linear time complexity. The main strength is its efficiency in both time and space, while the weakness is that it is not optimized for very large n beyond the given constraints.
